documentation.text:
@title AnonymousMail
 @notice On-chain anonymous mailbox. The headline privacy property is visible
         directly in the storage layout: **there is no sender field anywhere**.
         A user encrypts a message to the recipient's key, hands the sealed
         blob to an Acurast TEE relay (see ACURAST.md), and the relay submits
         it here. Because submission goes through a relayer set, `msg.sender`
         is the relay — never the author — so the author is unlinkable from
         the chain's perspective. The relay runs inside a hardware Trusted
         Execution Environment, so not even the relay operator learns the
         author. Content lives on the Bulletin Chain and is pruned at TTL.
         This contract is intentionally minimal and auditable: a reviewer can
         confirm in one read that no metadata can leak a sender.
@notice Authorized Acurast TEE relays allowed to deliver mail.

documentation.text:
@notice Account authorized to attest personhood (Individuality oracle).
         In production this is the bridge to Polkadot's Individuality pallet.
@notice Register or update the caller's encryption key + profile.
@notice Update only the profile CID (avatar/name on the Bulletin Chain).
@notice Personhood attestation from the Individuality oracle.

@title Individuality
 @notice On-chain mirror / consumer of Polkadot's **Project Individuality**
         (the People Chain), modeled as Gavin Wood imagined it:
         - **Contextual aliases.** A registered human derives a *different,
           unlinkable* alias per context (app/channel/service) via a ring-VRF.
           You never present the same pseudonym twice across contexts.
         - **Nullifiers.** Each (person, context) yields one deterministic
           nullifier, so a person can hold **at most one alias per context** —
           Sybil resistance without identity, without KYC.
         - **DIMs.** Assurance comes in levels: DIM1 (Proof of Individuality,
           uniqueness — fine for low-risk gating like channels) and DIM2
           (Proof of Verified Individuality — for high-value actions).
         - **People root.** `peopleRoot` commits to the set of registered
           unique humans on the People Chain, updated by an OpenGov-controlled
           bridge. The actual ring/zk lives on the People Chain; this contract
           verifies proofs against the root and tracks per-context aliases.
@notice OpenGov-controlled bridge that publishes the People Chain root.
@notice Commitment to the registered set of unique humans.

typeDescriptions.typeString: mapping(bytes32 => mapping(address => struct Individuality.Alias storage ref))
mapping(bytes32 => mapping(address => struct Individuality.Alias storage ref))

documentation.text:
@notice Voluntarily retire an alias from a context (you keep your humanity,
         not this pseudonym). The nullifier stays spent — no re-aliasing.

typeDescriptions.typeString: mapping(bytes32 => mapping(address => struct Individuality.Alias storage ref))
mapping(bytes32 => mapping(address => struct Individuality.Alias storage ref))

documentation.text:
@title Messenger
 @notice Core of Umbra messaging. The chain stores ONLY a pointer (the
         Bulletin Chain CID) to the E2E-encrypted payload plus the minimal
         metadata needed to reconstruct a conversation. The actual content is
         encrypted client-side and lives on the Bulletin Chain
         (write-to-chain, read-from-network).
         Disappearing messages: every message carries an `expiresAt`. This
         mirrors the Bulletin Chain retention model — the encrypted blob is
         pruned network-wide once its retention lapses (the Bulletin Chain is
         NOT immutable like other system parachains), so an expired message is
         genuinely unrecoverable, not merely hidden. `burn()` lets the sender
         retract the on-chain pointer early.
         Interconnection: Messenger queries IdentityRegistry to ensure the
         recipient published an encryption key, and Individuality to enforce
         per-context proof-of-personhood on gated channels — using the channel
         id itself as the Individuality *context*, so a member proves they are
         a unique human *for that channel* via an unlinkable contextual alias.
@notice Deterministic, symmetric id of a 1:1 conversation.
@notice Send a direct message. `ttlSeconds == 0` means no expiry.

documentation.text:
@title IIndividuality
 @notice Minimal view of Polkadot's Individuality system (the People Chain) as
         consumed by the rest of the Umbra network. Personhood is **per
         context**, never global: an account is a unique human *within a given
         context* via an unlinkable contextual alias. This is the whole point
         of Gavin Wood's design — counting unique people without linking their
         actions across services.
@notice True if `account` is a verified unique human in `context`.

destroys the on-chain pointer; the Bulletin blob is left         to lapse and is pruned at retention end."},"deliver(address,string,uint64)":{"notice":"Deliver sealed mail to `to`. Callable only by an authorized relay."},"relayer(address)":{"notice":"Authorized Acurast TEE relays allowed to deliver mail."}},"notice":"On-chain anonymous mailbox. The headline privacy property is visible         directly in the storage layout: **there is no sender field anywhere**.         A user encrypts a message to the recipient's key, hands the sealed         blob to an Acurast TEE relay (see ACURAST.md), and the relay submits         it here. Because submission goes through a relayer set, `msg.sender`         is the relay \u2014 never the author \u2014 so the author is unlinkable from         the chain's perspective. The relay runs inside a hardware Trusted         Execution Environment, so not even the relay operator learns the         author. Content lives on the Bulletin Chain and is pruned at TTL.         This contract is intentionally minimal and auditable: a reviewer can         confirm in one read that no metadata can leak a sender.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/AnonymousMail.sol":"AnonymousMail"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/AnonymousMail.sol":{"keccak256":"0xfdb139e141dd2eb788f3a832243b139d2657c3ead49872eb74ce3ca0be0c298f","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://d4d660de269e98791f83af5ef82bee58c33cb9b48fb3502c414aee17dae96a96","dweb:/ipfs/QmPdapByTgN7mMZ4wHefZxLi1y6cDubs6udA2ZqnoSaA2N"]},"contracts/interfaces/IIdentityRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x05b9662106d67ef07909d546fd4e417127c77baf7f8977bcb74af86698f6aa5d","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://8e1db3f5a84278e0853621c4ef62775fa1122e8c5fe2a30cb9e26b740f438ece","dweb:/ipfs/QmZ8pDdaedc9Zwr7o2odR82zvafVmB5mpmUtCvgy2cA5vt"]}},"version":1}

uts":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"peopleChainBridge","outputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"peopleRoot","outputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"","type":"bytes32"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"context","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"nullifier","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"uint8","name":"dim","type":"uint8"},{"internalType":"bytes","name":"proof","type":"bytes"}],"name":"registerAlias","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"context","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"revokeAlias","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"bridge","type":"address"}],"name":"setBridge","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"root","type":"bytes32"}],"name":"setPeopleRoot","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"address","name":"ringVrfVerifier","type":"address"}],"name":"setVerifier","outputs":[],"stateMutability":"nonpayable","type":"function"},{"inputs":[],"name":"verifier","outputs":[{"internalType":"contract IRingVrfVerifier","name":"","type":"address"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"kind":"dev","methods":{"registerAlias(bytes32,bytes32,uint8,bytes)":{"params":{"dim":"Requested assurance level (1 or 2); the proof must support it."}}},"title":"Individuality","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"dimOf(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"DIM (Decentralized Individuality Mechanism) assurance level held         by `account` in `context`: 0 = none, 1 = Proof of Individuality,         2 = Proof of Verified Individuality."},"isUnique(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"True if `account` is a verified unique human in `context`."},"peopleChainBridge()":{"notice":"OpenGov-controlled bridge that publishes the People Chain root."},"peopleRoot()":{"notice":"Commitment to the registered set of unique humans."},"registerAlias(bytes32,bytes32,uint8,bytes)":{"notice":"Register a contextual alias. `msg.sender` is the freshly-derived,         unlinkable alias account for `context`. `nullifier` is the ring-VRF         output bound to (person, context); it is deterministic per person,         so a second registration in the same context is rejected."},"revokeAlias(bytes32)":{"notice":"Voluntarily retire an alias from a context (you keep your humanity,         not this pseudonym). The nullifier stays spent \u2014 no re-aliasing."}},"notice":"On-chain mirror / consumer of Polkadot's **Project Individuality**         (the People Chain), modeled as Gavin Wood imagined it:         - **Contextual aliases.** A registered human derives a *different,           unlinkable* alias per context (app/channel/service) via a ring-VRF.           You never present the same pseudonym twice across contexts.         - **Nullifiers.** Each (person, context) yields one deterministic           nullifier, so a person can hold **at most one alias per context** \u2014           Sybil resistance without identity, without KYC.         - **DIMs.** Assurance comes in levels: DIM1 (Proof of Individuality,           uniqueness \u2014 fine for low-risk gating like channels) and DIM2           (Proof of Verified Individuality \u2014 for high-value actions).         - **People root.** `peopleRoot` commits to the set of registered           unique humans on the People Chain, updated by an OpenGov-controlled           bridge. The actual ring/zk lives on the People Chain; this contract           verifies proofs against the root and tracks per-context aliases.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/Individuality.sol":"Individuality"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/Individuality.sol":{"keccak256":"0x61909234a9f68e3a448abe76edfdc65b1baaf5fd119888869937f53ed25032e1","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://e5962f0e583513b82fcdd248ed19f9da4d733909ed7660b9a4812a1b93431e8e","dweb:/ipfs/QmegNup4xiu1f7RxpnkWsY5E7LC2pH6u2YVbwTxxpbeqBD"]},"contracts/interfaces/IIndividuality.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe2d3c2c3aea29583f8f4c3d2a045280036a621e2a90bda28363a44eec9e5f5f3","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://c727af6059a992fe58f37807f645115b395003d36c3f24266f87ff0dc2278df9","dweb:/ipfs/QmeFMopWcMaTUEXLwr3xcJ7J6SC2PuHQxX3crDEQGS8Zkx"]}},"version":1}

a message early. Off-chain, the client also drops the         Bulletin renewal so the encrypted blob is pruned at retention end."},"dmId(address,address)":{"notice":"Deterministic, symmetric id of a 1:1 conversation."},"sendDirect(address,string,uint64)":{"notice":"Send a direct message. `ttlSeconds == 0` means no expiry."}},"notice":"Core of Umbra messaging. The chain stores ONLY a pointer (the         Bulletin Chain CID) to the E2E-encrypted payload plus the minimal         metadata needed to reconstruct a conversation. The actual content is         encrypted client-side and lives on the Bulletin Chain         (write-to-chain, read-from-network).         Disappearing messages: every message carries an `expiresAt`. This         mirrors the Bulletin Chain retention model \u2014 the encrypted blob is         pruned network-wide once its retention lapses (the Bulletin Chain is         NOT immutable like other system parachains), so an expired message is         genuinely unrecoverable, not merely hidden. `burn()` lets the sender         retract the on-chain pointer early.         Interconnection: Messenger queries IdentityRegistry to ensure the         recipient published an encryption key, and Individuality to enforce         per-context proof-of-personhood on gated channels \u2014 using the channel         id itself as the Individuality *context*, so a member proves they are         a unique human *for that channel* via an unlinkable contextual alias.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/Messenger.sol":"Messenger"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/Messenger.sol":{"keccak256":"0x844c2e3698b02fbba2a1d4c84f073d378fd7ccbddfc8418c3e0336092feef408","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://12a69c44118a9733b969a0efa360efa5060463d6c7631e7febfc0d665fb024a7","dweb:/ipfs/QmXn2sPVkB3ne44mUTNHTqsUxYNxq7uZRSoiphiyFzdPrh"]},"contracts/interfaces/IIdentityRegistry.sol":{"keccak256":"0x05b9662106d67ef07909d546fd4e417127c77baf7f8977bcb74af86698f6aa5d","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://8e1db3f5a84278e0853621c4ef62775fa1122e8c5fe2a30cb9e26b740f438ece","dweb:/ipfs/QmZ8pDdaedc9Zwr7o2odR82zvafVmB5mpmUtCvgy2cA5vt"]},"contracts/interfaces/IIndividuality.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe2d3c2c3aea29583f8f4c3d2a045280036a621e2a90bda28363a44eec9e5f5f3","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://c727af6059a992fe58f37807f645115b395003d36c3f24266f87ff0dc2278df9","dweb:/ipfs/QmeFMopWcMaTUEXLwr3xcJ7J6SC2PuHQxX3crDEQGS8Zkx"]}},"version":1}

IIndividuality.metadata: {"compiler":{"version":"0.8.26+commit.8a97fa7a"},"language":"Solidity","output":{"abi":[{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"context","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"address","name":"account","type":"address"}],"name":"dimOf","outputs":[{"internalType":"uint8","name":"","type":"uint8"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"},{"inputs":[{"internalType":"bytes32","name":"context","type":"bytes32"},{"internalType":"address","name":"account","type":"address"}],"name":"isUnique","outputs":[{"internalType":"bool","name":"","type":"bool"}],"stateMutability":"view","type":"function"}],"devdoc":{"kind":"dev","methods":{},"title":"IIndividuality","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"dimOf(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"DIM (Decentralized Individuality Mechanism) assurance level held         by `account` in `context`: 0 = none, 1 = Proof of Individuality,         2 = Proof of Verified Individuality."},"isUnique(bytes32,address)":{"notice":"True if `account` is a verified unique human in `context`."}},"notice":"Minimal view of Polkadot's Individuality system (the People Chain) as         consumed by the rest of the Umbra network. Personhood is **per         context**, never global: an account is a unique human *within a given         context* via an unlinkable contextual alias. This is the whole point         of Gavin Wood's design \u2014 counting unique people without linking their         actions across services.","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"contracts/interfaces/IIndividuality.sol":"IIndividuality"},"evmVersion":"paris","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[]},"sources":{"contracts/interfaces/IIndividuality.sol":{"keccak256":"0xe2d3c2c3aea29583f8f4c3d2a045280036a621e2a90bda28363a44eec9e5f5f3","license":"UNLICENSED","urls":["bzz-raw://c727af6059a992fe58f37807f645115b395003d36c3f24266f87ff0dc2278df9","dweb:/ipfs/QmeFMopWcMaTUEXLwr3xcJ7J6SC2PuHQxX3crDEQGS8Zkx"]}},"version":1}
